hero.desc1: In Quechua, Umiña means a single precious stone, while Achala means jewelry crafted from many stones.
hero.desc2: Umiña Achala unites Peru's stones, minerals and jewelry with immutable records, authentic origins, and artisan storytelling. Every piece is traced from mine to craft, ensuring compliance, provenance, and cultural heritage.

about.title: About Umina Achala
about.whatDesc1: Umina Achala unites stone provenance with artisanal jewelry. It bridges compliance, heritage, and digital provenance, creating a transparent marketplace for stones (Umina) and jewelry (Achala originating from Peru).
about.whatDesc2: The project is built on the Hedera blockchain, chosen for its speed, low fees, and energy efficiency. Hedera's distributed ledger ensures that every stone and jewelry record is immutable, auditable, and verifiable. This means provenance data, artisan recognition, and compliance identifiers are securely stored and accessible worldwide.
about.uminaDesc: Umina represents the certified stone (or mineral in solid form) in raw, cut or mounted form. Each stone is documented with provenance records, ensuring authenticity and compliance.
about.achalaDesc: Achala represents artisanal jewelry crafted from tokenized stones and minerals. While Umina certifies provenance, Achala transforms these stones into culturally significant creations that honor artisans and preserve heritage.
about.complianceDesc: Every Umina and Achala piece is anchored in Real World Assets (RWAs), ensuring verifiability and compliance with Peruvian regulations.
about.platformDesc: The Umina Achala Online Platform is the digital hub where provenance, compliance, and artistry converge. It provides collectors, artisans, and reviewers with transparent access to the lifecycle of stones and jewelry.
walletItems: Users connect Hedera-compatible wallets to authenticate and transact.
Wallets enable secure acquisition of Umina (stone NFTs) and Achala (jewelry NFTs).
provenanceItems: Each NFT includes metadata: concession ID, REINFO ID, artisan RNA, and vendor RUC.
Buyers can trace the origin of stones and the artisanship of jewelry.
Provenance records are immutable and verifiable on-chain.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: Umina Achala is a cultural‑tech project that unites stone provenance with artisanal jewelry. It bridges compliance, heritage, and digital provenance, creating a transparent marketplace where certified stones (Umina) are elevated into jewelry (Achala).
faq.a2: Umina represents certified stones — raw, cut, or mounted — documented with provenance records. Each Umina NFT ensures authenticity, compliance with Peruvian law, and immutable proof of origin.
faq.a3: Achala is the jewelry counterpart to Umina. While Umina certifies individual stones, Achala represents composite artisanal jewelry, blending multiple stones into culturally significant creations while preserving provenance and recognizing craftsmanship.
faq.q4: How does Umina Achala ensure compliance?
faq.a4: Each NFT embeds legal identifiers such as Concession ID (INGEMMET), REINFO ID, Vendor RUC (SUNAT), and Artisan RNA. These details are notarized on the Hedera Consensus Service, making provenance immutable and verifiable.
faq.a5: Hedera provides speed, low fees, and energy efficiency. Its distributed ledger ensures that provenance, compliance, and artisan recognition are securely stored, auditable, and globally accessible. Smart contracts guarantee royalties flow directly to artisans.
faq.q6: Can I buy Umina or Achala NFTs?
faq.a6: Yes. Buyers can connect Hedera‑compatible wallets to the platform and purchase NFTs directly. Transactions are transparent, and royalties are automatically routed to artisans, ensuring fair recognition.
faq.q7: What makes Umina Achala NFTs unique?
faq.a7: Beyond compliance and provenance, Umina Achala NFTs capture artistry — mount type, artisan recognition, techniques, and composite jewelry creations. This ensures both legal transparency and cultural appreciation.

marketplace.noResults: No stones match your filters. Try adjusting your search.

privacy.title: Privacy & Cookies
privacy.policyDesc: Umina Achala values your privacy. We collect only the information necessary to provide secure transactions, verify compliance, and support artisans. Personal data is never sold or shared with third‑parties outside the ecosystem.
policyItems: Wallet addresses are used solely for authentication and transactions.
Compliance identifiers (Concession ID, REINFO ID, RUC, RNA) are stored immutably on Hedera.
Metadata is linked to NFTs for provenance and transparency.
privacy.cookiesDesc: Our platform uses cookies to improve user experience and ensure secure access. Cookies help us remember preferences, maintain session integrity, and analyze platform usage.
privacy.essential: Essential Cookies: Required for login, wallet connection, and secure navigation.
privacy.preference: Preference Cookies: Store language, display, and accessibility settings.
privacy.analytics: Analytics Cookies: Provide insights into platform usage to improve services.
privacy.rightsDesc: You may request access to your data, ask for corrections, or request deletion where applicable. Blockchain records are immutable, but off‑chain data such as preferences can be updated or removed.

compliance.cutTypeDesc: Raw, Cut, or Mounted state of the stone.
compliance.txIdDesc: Hedera Transaction ID anchoring compliance proof.
compliance.exportStatusDesc: Export lifecycle state (Pending, Verified, Restricted, Not Applicable).
compliance.lastUpdatedDesc: Timestamp of the latest compliance check.
